mission.title: contour: Address not set on Ingress resource
mission.description: Address not set on Ingress resource. Requested by 16+ users.

steps.title: Check current contour deployment
steps.description:
Verify your contour version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n projectcontour -l app.kubernetes.io/name=contour
helm list -n projectcontour 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working contour installation.

steps.title: Review contour configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant contour configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n projectcontour -l app.kubernetes.io/name=contour
kubectl get configmap -n projectcontour -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=contour
```
Contour does not set the IP value on the Ingress resource. This value is necessary for services like [ExternalDNS](https://github.com/kubernetes-incubator/external-dns) to work properly.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Address not set on Ingress resource
steps.description:
I was going to pick this up, my thought here was to do the following:

1. Contour watches for all Services right now, when it finds service with an external LB attached, then look to see if that matches the namespace where Contour is deployed. This will be the IP/DNS name that should get attached
```yaml
but has value:
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n projectcontour -l app.kubernetes.io/name=contour
kubectl get events -n projectcontour --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Address not set on Ingress resource" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
I was going to pick this up, my thought here was to do the following:

1. Contour watches for all Services right now, when it finds service with an external LB attached, then look to see if that matches the namespace where Contour is deployed. This will be the IP/DNS name that should get attached to any Ingress object.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with contour installed or the issue environment reproducible.
